edges.evidence_quote: Originating in the Jaish al-Ta'ifa al-Mansurah founded by Abu Omar al-Baghdadi in 2004
edges.context: Islamic State originated from Jaish al-Ta'ifa al-Mansurah.

edges.evidence_quote: Originating in the Jaish al-Ta'ifa al-Mansurah founded by Abu Omar al-Baghdadi in 2004, the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency.
edges.context: The Islamic State originated from Jaish al-Ta'ifa al-Mansurah, which was its predecessor.

edges.evidence_quote: the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency.
edges.context: The Islamic State (as Islamic State of Iraq) was affiliated with al-Qaeda and fought alongside them.

edges.evidence_quote: the Islamic State claimed that al-Husseini al-Qurashi was killed in a shootout with Tahrir al-Sham (HTS) forces.
edges.context: Islamic State and Tahrir al-Sham were in conflict, resulting in the death of an IS leader.

edges.evidence_quote: The financial support from Al Qaeda came after Zarqawi was reluctant to be recruited by bin Laden.
edges.context: Al-Qaeda provided financial support to JTJ, the precursor to IS.

edges.evidence_quote: the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency.
edges.context: Islamic State of Iraq fought alongside al-Qaeda in Iraq during the insurgency.

edges.evidence_quote: the media wing of IS's all-female Al-Khansaa Brigade
edges.context: Al-Khansaa Brigade is the all-female media wing of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Central Africa Province (IS-CAP) first appeared in 2018 and operates in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda since 2020. It emerged out of the Ugandan Islamist group Allied Democratic Forces (ADF).
edges.context: IS-CAP is an official affiliate of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: It emerged out of the Ugandan Islamist group Allied Democratic Forces (ADF).
edges.context: IS-CAP emerged from the ADF.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Mozambique Province (ISMP), formerly part of Islamic State – Central Africa Province (IS-CAP), was formed in 2022.
edges.context: ISMP is an official affiliate of IS, formerly part of IS-CAP.

edges.evidence_quote: It emerged out of the Mozambican Islamist group Al-Shabaab.
edges.context: ISMP emerged from the Mozambican Al-Shabaab group.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Sahel Province (ISSP), formerly known as Islamic State in the Greater Sahara (IS-GS), operates mainly in Mali, Niger and Burkina Faso.
edges.context: ISSP is an official affiliate of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: From March 2019 to 2022, IS-GS was formally part of the Islamic State – West Africa Province (ISWAP); when it was also called "ISWAP-Greater Sahara".
edges.context: IS-GS was formally part of ISWAP from 2019 to 2022.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – West Africa Province (ISWAP) is primarily active in the Chad Basin of Niger, Nigeria, Cameroon and Chad. It emerged in 2015 after a split from Boko Haram.
edges.context: ISWAP is an official affiliate of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: It emerged in 2015 after a split from Boko Haram.
edges.context: ISWAP split from Boko Haram.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Somalia Province (ISSP) operates primarily in Puntland in northern Somalia. The group first appeared in 2015 when elements within the Somali Islamist group Al-Shabaab defected and pledged allegiance to IS.
edges.context: ISSP is an official affiliate of IS, formed by defectors from Al-Shabaab.

edges.evidence_quote: elements within the Somali Islamist group Al-Shabaab defected and pledged allegiance to IS.
edges.context: ISSP formed from defectors of Al-Shabaab.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Azerbaijan Province (IS-AP) emerged in 2019 and operates in Azerbaijan. Its adeherents were previously recruited by both the Islamic State – Caucasus Province (IS-CP) and Islamic State – Khorasan Province (ISIS-K).
edges.context: IS-AP is an official affiliate of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: Its adeherents were previously recruited by both the Islamic State – Caucasus Province (IS-CP) and Islamic State – Khorasan Province (ISIS-K).
edges.context: IS-AP's adherents were recruited by IS-CP.

edges.evidence_quote: Its adeherents were previously recruited by both the Islamic State – Caucasus Province (IS-CP) and Islamic State – Khorasan Province (ISIS-K).
edges.context: IS-AP's adherents were recruited by ISIS-K.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Caucasus Province (IS-CP) is active in the North Caucasus region of Russia. IS announced the group's formation in 2015.
edges.context: IS-CP is an official affiliate of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Khorasan Province (ISIS-K) emerged in 2015 and operates in Central and South Asia.
edges.context: ISIS-K is an official affiliate of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Bengal Province (IS-BP) is active in Bangladesh. The group is an offshoot of the Al-Qaeda affiliated Jamaat-ul-Mujahideen Bangladesh (JMB). It emerged in 2015 and was claimed by IS in 2016.
edges.context: IS-BP is an official affiliate of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: The group is an offshoot of the Al-Qaeda affiliated Jamaat-ul-Mujahideen Bangladesh (JMB).
edges.context: IS-BP is an offshoot of JMB.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Hind Province (ISHP) is the Indian branch of IS and operates in Indian Jammu and Kashmir. Its formation was announced in 2019.
edges.context: ISHP is an official affiliate of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Pakistan Province (ISPP) was formed in 2019 as a split from Islamic State – Khorasan Province( ISIS-K).
edges.context: ISPP is an official affiliate of IS, split from ISIS-K.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Pakistan Province (ISPP) was formed in 2019 as a split from Islamic State – Khorasan Province( ISIS-K).
edges.context: ISPP split from ISIS-K.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Philippines Province (ISPP) operates in the Philippines, primarily in Mindanao. It emerged in 2016 from local jihadist groups Maute Group, Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters (BIFF), and Ansar Khalifa Philippines (AKP).
edges.context: ISPP is an official affiliate of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: It emerged in 2016 from local jihadist groups Maute Group, Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters (BIFF), and Ansar Khalifa Philippines (AKP).
edges.context: ISPP emerged from Maute Group.

edges.evidence_quote: It emerged in 2016 from local jihadist groups Maute Group, Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters (BIFF), and Ansar Khalifa Philippines (AKP).
edges.context: ISPP emerged from BIFF.

edges.evidence_quote: It emerged in 2016 from local jihadist groups Maute Group, Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters (BIFF), and Ansar Khalifa Philippines (AKP).
edges.context: ISPP emerged from AKP.

edges.evidence_quote: Islamic State – Turkey Province (IS-TP) was formed in 2019 and is active in Turkey.
edges.context: IS-TP is an official affiliate of IS.

edges.evidence_quote: In February 2014, Ayman al-Zawahiri, the leader of Al-Qaeda, announced that his group Al-Qaeda had cut ties with the Islamic State of Iraq and the Levant and denounced ISIL after being unable to reconcile a conflict between them and the al-Qaeda affiliate al-Nusra Front.
edges.context: Al-Qaeda cut ties with ISIL due to conflict over al-Nusra Front.

edges.evidence_quote: Al-Nusra and ISIL are both hostile towards the Druze. However, while al-Nusra has typically destroyed Druze shrines and pressured them to convert to Sunni Islam, ISIL regards the entire Druze community as a valid target for violence, as it does the Yazidis.
edges.context: Al-Nusra and ISIL are hostile towards each other, with differing approaches to Druze.

edges.evidence_quote: The Jaysh al-Islam group within the Islamic Front criticised ISIL, saying: 'They killed the people of Islam and leave the idol worshippers ... They use the verses talking about the disbelievers and implement it on the Muslims'.
edges.context: Jaysh al-Islam criticized ISIL for killing Muslims.

edges.evidence_quote: In February 2014, after continued tensions, al-Qaeda publicly disavowed any relations with ISIL.
edges.context: Al-Qaeda disavowed relations with ISIL in February 2014, indicating a split.

edges.evidence_quote: ISIL and al-Nusra Front still cooperate with each other occasionally when they fight against the Syrian government.
edges.context: ISIL and al-Nusra Front occasionally cooperate against the Syrian government.

edges.evidence_quote: the Taliban banned all Afghans from associating with the local Khorasan Province branch of IS in July 2022, and labelled it a 'false sect'.
edges.context: Taliban banned association with IS's Khorasan branch, indicating conflict.
